item.title: 'World cup pubs boost' and 'Kate holds court'
item.description: Late openings of pubs and Catherine appearing at Wimbledon lead Friday's front pages.

item.title: How the American Dream has survived 250 years, but only just
item.description: The American Dream has persisted since the days of the Founding Fathers, but faith in the ideal is fading

item.title: Photos: Fans gather in the Bay Area for the World Cup match between the U.S. and Bosnia-Herzegovina
item.description: World Cup watch parties in Santa Clara and the San Francisco Bay Area brought fans together for the United States vs. Bosnia-Herzegovina match, where the U.S. earned a 2-0 win to reach the Round of 16

item.title: Rare copy of US Declaration of Independence found by volunteer in UK archives
item.description: The newly discovered copy is one of only 11 of its kind in the world.

item.title: Weekly quiz: Why did Catherine climb the UK's three highest peaks?
item.description: How much attention did you pay to what happened in the world over the past seven days?

item.title: Chancellor's message to Germany's World Cup exit backfires
item.description: "You've inspired us," read a post by Chancellor Merz on X right after Germany's lackluster performance and early elimination from the FIFA World Cup. It is seen as a communication blunder that has rep

item.title: Physicists Discover Superconducting States That Get Stronger Under Conditions Meant to Kill Them
item.description: Graphene keeps getting weirder... ScienceAlert stories are written, fact-checked, and edited by humans, never generated by AI. Don't miss a story, subscribe here.

item.title: Comet From Outside Our Solar System Has Chemistry Unlike Anything We've Ever Seen Before
item.description: What are we looking at here? ScienceAlert stories are written, fact-checked, and edited by humans, never generated by AI. Don't miss a story, subscribe here.

item.title: First-of-Its-Kind Lupus Treatment Significantly Reduces Symptoms in Mice
item.description: New hope for millions with the disease. ScienceAlert stories are written, fact-checked, and edited by humans, never generated by AI. Don't miss a story, subscribe here.

item.title: Extreme heat advisory delays National Mall July 4 event, organizers say
item.description: Entry to the National Mall for a record-breaking fireworks show celebrating July 4 has been delayed as Washington, D.C. braces for extreme heat, Freedom 250 organizers said Thursday. President Trump i

item.title: Ronaldo scores as Portugal come back to win, Croatia denied by late VAR
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo pulls Portugal level before Goncalo Ramos wins it late on; Croatia have further goal ruled offside.

item.title: Who are the rogue bishops defying Pope Leo XIV?
item.description: A rebel group of Roman Catholics has ordained its own bishops in direct defiance of Pope Leo XIV and the Vatican order.

item.title: ‘They gave their best’: Congolese reflect on historic World Cup run
item.description: The Leopards' first World Cup in more than 50 years united Congolese supporters, despite defeat to England.

item.title: Venezuela leader rejects criticism for earthquake response
item.description: Acting President Delcy Rodríguez has slammed critics for saying that her government was slow to respond to the disaster. She said the criticism was "manufactured in propaganda laboratories."

item.title: Moment huge waterspout twirls over lake in Germany
item.description: The phenomenon formed over a Lake Constance near the southern German city of Friedrichshafen.

item.title: Canada’s Carney secures deal for pipeline to expand oil exports beyond US
item.description: Ottawa says future pipeline will reduce economic dependence on the US amid Donald Trump's trade war.

item.title: In Photos: Check out some of the best World Cup fancy dress
item.description: World Cup 2026 fans show off their excitement with creative costumes inspired by national symbols, culture and football traditions.

item.title: US gets ready to celebrate 250th birthday
item.description: The United States of America turns 250 this year, so we've had a look at the history of US independence and what people and organisations around the UK are doing to mark this huge milestone.

item.title: 500-million-year-old fossil explains spider's bite
item.description: Experts say the discovery could help them better understand how life was evolving on our planet at that time. Find out more here.

item.title: As heat waves loom, scientists wonder how humans will adapt
item.description: Temperatures are set to soar in the US around the July 4 holiday, a week after Europe struck new records. With experts predicting more intense and more frequent heat waves in the future, can our bodie
